metadata.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) → AppSec Core V1 (with ACR-001, ACR-002, ACR-004 incorporated) (Concept Crosswalk)
metadata.schema-basis: NIST IR 8477 relationship vocabulary + NIST IR 8278A r1 STRM resource model

source-document.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
source-document.author: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology

reference-document.title: AppSec Core V1 (with ACR-001, ACR-002, ACR-004 incorporated)
reference-document.author: SbD-ToE Programme — AppSec Core ontology project

metadata.methodology: Threshold-based continuous→categorical translation: sim≥0.6 → equal; 0.4≤sim<0.6 → intersects-with; sim<0.4 → unspecified. Multi-claim per source-item: one row per (source-item, target-Core-entity) unique pair using highest-similarity claim for that target. is-primary="true" marks item overall highest-similarity claim's target. subset-of / superset-of NOT in first cut (cosine similarity lacks directional info; SME refinement future work). STRM resource model: pilot doc → DocumentaryImpl; source-item → ConcreteImpl; AppSec Core V1 → Reference Document; Core entity → Concept.

source-element.description: GOVERN 1.1: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI. Policies, processes, are understood, managed, and documented. procedures, and GOVERN 1.2: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are inte- p…

reference-element.description: Governed Promotion And Release Approval. Separate security signal generation from approval decisions and enforce explicit release gates. policy and gate enforcement. approval

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.469; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim NIST_AI_RMF_1_0-CLAIM-000001 at Practice level. Primary anchor (item highest-similarity claim).

source-element.description: GOVERN 1.1: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI. Policies, processes, are understood, managed, and documented. procedures, and GOVERN 1.2: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are inte- p…

reference-element.description: Secret Configuration Governance. Keep secret-bearing configuration reviewable, permissioned and attributable through controlled governance. governance and review. governance

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.465; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim NIST_AI_RMF_1_0-CLAIM-000002 at Practice level. Secondary disclosure.

source-element.description: GOVERN 1.1: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI. Policies, processes, are understood, managed, and documented. procedures, and GOVERN 1.2: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are inte- p…

reference-element.description: Least-Privilege Authorization Governance. Keep permission models explicit, separated by role or attribute and bounded by least-privilege rules. identity and trust control. authorization

reference-element.description: Release Promotion And Human Approval Integrity. Promotion between environments must respect explicit gates, including human approval for irreversible actions. Promotion gates are enforced and irrever…

source-element.description: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place. measuring, and to determine the needed level of risk management activities based managing of AI on the organization’s risk tolerance. risks are in p…

reference-element.description: Structured Threat Analysis Frameworks. Apply an explicit threat-analysis method with minimum coverage discipline appropriate to the risk context. governance and review. modeling framework

source-element.description: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems. and are resourced according to organizational risk priorities.
